chunk_summary: Guidelines for safe formula preparation, hygiene, and feeding techniques, including cup feeding, to prevent infections, support nutrition, and protect breastfeeding in infants under 2 years.
learning_objectives: Understand safe preparation and storage of powdered infant formula to minimize bacterial contamination.
Learn proper feeding techniques, including cup feeding, to reduce health risks and support bonding.
Identify strategies to protect breastfeeding during mixed feeding and monitor infant growth effectively.
key_facts: Boil water and cool to ~70°C before mixing powdered formula to reduce bacterial contamination.
Prepared formula should be refrigerated immediately and discarded after 24 hours if unused.
Over-dilution of formula can cause hyponatremia and poor weight gain, while over-concentration increases renal solute load.
Cup or spoon feeding is preferred over bottles in areas with poor sanitation to reduce infection risks.
By the end of the first year, transition from bottles to cups to reduce dental caries and support oral-motor development.
reference_quote: Use potable water; boil and cool to ~70°C before mixing powdered formula to reduce bacterial contamination. (WHO, 2007)
document_key_topics: Safe preparation and storage of infant formula
Techniques for cup feeding and paced bottle feeding
